questions.question: Which of the following articles of Indian Constitution ensures the protection of minority interest?
questions.explanation: Article 29 protects the cultural and educational rights of minorities. It ensures that any section of citizens having a distinct language, script, or culture has the right to conserve it. Article 28 deals with religious instruction in educational institutions. Article 27 prohibits compelling any person to pay taxes for promotion of any particular religion. Article 26 gives freedom to manage religious affairs to all religious denominations, not specifically minorities.

questions.question: Which of the following statement is Incorrect about the Indian Desert?
options: Vegetation is sparse and consists mainly of xerophytic plants
Sand dunes and arid climate are typical features of this region
It lies mainly in the northwestern part of India.
Red soil is common in the Desert due to wind deposition and sand dunes.
questions.explanation: Statement (D) is incorrect: The Indian Desert mainly has sandy and saline soils. Red soil is not characteristic of desert regions.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is/are correct regarding Asian Athletics Championship 2025? • It was held in Gumi, South Korea from 27 to 31 May 2025 • India finished top in the medal tally with highest medals. • Gulveer Singh won Gold medal in the men’s 10,000 m event.
questions.explanation: Statement 2 is incorrect as China finished 1st with 19 gold medals (32 total medals) and India finished 2nd with 24 medals (8 gold). Statements 1 and 3 are correct. India : Total medals: 24 Gold: 8, Silver: 10, Bronze: 6

questions.question: Which of the following statement is incorrect about periodic table?
options: Atomic size decreases across a period from left to right
Ionization energy increases down a group
Atomic size increases down a group.
questions.explanation: Statement 2 is incorrect as Ionization energy decreases down a group. Other statements are correct.

questions.question: Which of the following person is not associated with Kirana Gharana?
questions.explanation: Gangubai Hangal, Prabha Atre, and Firoz Dastur are all prominent exponents of the Kirana Gharana. Kishori Amonkar belonged to the Jaipur-Atrauli Gharana, not the Kirana Gharana Kirana Gharana Abdul Karim Khan – Founder of Kirana Gharana Abdul Wahid Khan – Co-founder Bhimsen Joshi – Bharat Ratna awardee Gangubai Hangal – Renowned female exponent Prabha Atre – Famous vocalist and scholar Firoz Dastur – Noted exponent Sawai Gandharva – Guru of Bhimsen Joshi Key feature: Focus on swara (notes) purity and slow, detailed elaboration Jaipur-Atrauli Gharana Alladiya Khan – Founder Kesarbai Kerkar – Legendary singer Mogubai Kurdikar – Famous exponent Kishori Amonkar – Very popular classical singer Mallikarjun Mansur – Known for rare ragas Ashwini Bhide Deshpande – Contemporary artist Key feature: Complex ragas, intricate layakari (rhythm), and rare compositions

questions.question: During the Delhi Sultanate period, which of the following taxes was levied on treasure looted in war?
questions.explanation: Khums was a tax on war booty (loot from battles). One-fifth of the total loot was taken by the state. Kharaz was a land tax imposed on agricultural land. Jizyah was a tax imposed on non-Muslims. Zakat was a religious tax paid by Muslims for charitable purposes.

questions.question: Match the following: Column –I (Art forms) Column-II (Relative State) A) Kalamkari 1) Uttrakhand B) Aipan Art 2) Rajasthan C) Phulkari 3) AndhraPradesh D) Phad Painting 4) Punjab
questions.explanation: Kalamkari is a famous textile painting tradition of India, mainly developed in Andhra Pradesh. The word Kalamkari is derived from two words—kalam meaning pen and kari meaning craftsmanship. In this art form, pictures are hand-painted on cloth with the help of natural dyes. Traditionally, scenes from the Ramayana, Mahabharata, and other mythological stories are depicted. The regions of Srikakulam and Machilipatnam in Andhra Pradesh have been the major centres of this art. Aipan Art is a traditional folk painting of Uttarakhand. It is mainly made in the courtyards, doorways, and places of worship in houses. In this art form, beautiful geometric and religious patterns are drawn with rice paste on a red clay background. This art is especially created during religious festivals, marriages, and other auspicious occasions, and it forms an important part of the cultural identity of Uttarakhand. Phulkari is a famous traditional embroidery art of Punjab. The word Phulkari means floral embroidery. In this craft, colourful threads are used to create beautiful floral and geometric designs on cloth. Traditionally, women of Punjab practised this art on dupattas, shawls, and garments. Phulkari holds special significance during marriages and other social occasions. Phad Painting is a traditional folk painting of Rajasthan. In this art form, stories of folk deities and heroic figures are depicted through paintings on long pieces of cloth or canvas. It is mainly associated with the legends of folk deities such as Pabuji and Devnarayan. Traditionally, these paintings were used by Bhopas (traditional priest-singers) during the narration of these stories.

questions.question: Who is the author of the book “Sleeping on Jupiter”
questions.explanation: “Sleeping on Jupiter” is a novel written by Anuradha Roy. It was shortlisted for the Man Booker Prize in 2015 and explores themes of trauma, memory, and spirituality.

questions.question: The census of India is conducted under the provision of the Census Act _______ .
questions.explanation: The Census of India is conducted under the Census Act, 1948, which provides the legal framework for conducting population census in the country.

questions.question: Dekhni is a famous dance performed in which of the following Indian state?
questions.explanation: Dekhni is a traditional folk dance of Goa, performed mainly by women. It reflects a blend of Indian and Portuguese cultural influences and is usually accompanied by songs describing village life and devotion.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is/are true regarding World Air Quality report 2024. • India rank 3rd amongst the most polluted countries of the word. • Delhi is ranked as the most polluted capital city in the world • As per the reports, Only 17% of cities meet WHO standards
questions.explanation: Statement 1: Incorrect – India was the fifth most polluted country in 2024, not the third. Statement 2: Correct – According to the report, Delhi remains the world's most polluted capital city. Statement 3: Correct – Only approximately 17% of global cities meet the World Health Organization (WHO) air quality standards.

questions.question: With reference to the ordinance making power of the president of the India, which of the following statements is/are true? • Court cannot review it. • It cannot be implemented retrospectively like laws passed by the parliament. • It must be approved by Parliament within six weeks of reassembly
questions.explanation: The ordinance-making power is vested in the President of India under Article 123. Statement 1: Court cannot review it. Incorrect. Ordinances are subject to judicial review. In D.C. Wadhwa v. State of Bihar, the Supreme Court held that misuse of ordinance power can be challenged. Statement 2: It cannot be implemented retrospectively. Incorrect. Ordinances have the same force as laws and can be retrospective. Statement 3: It must be approved by Parliament within six weeks of reassembly. Correct. An ordinance must be laid before Parliament and will cease to operate after six weeks from reassembly if not approved.

questions.question: Read the following statements carefully and choose the correct option regarding Assertion (A) and Reason (R). Assertion (A) : Tax reforms post 1991 included lowering tax rates. Reason (R) : Lower tax rates believed to increase tax compliance and reduce evasion.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true, but R is false
A is false, but R is true
questions.explanation: Assertion (A): True. After the 1991 economic reforms in India, the government reduced high tax rates as part of liberalization and simplification of the tax system. Reason (R): True. Lower tax rates are expected to improve compliance, broaden the tax base, and reduce tax evasion. Link between A and R: The reduction in tax rates was done partly to encourage compliance and reduce evasion, so the reason correctly explains the assertion.

questions.question: The Aadi Karmyogi initiative has been launched by which of the following ministry?
options: Ministry of social justice and Empowerment
Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
questions.explanation: The Aadi Karmyogi initiative is a skill development program aimed at empowering tribal youth with employable skills and improving livelihood opportunities. It has been launched by the Ministry of Tribal Affairs.

questions.question: Which of the following plant tissues provide flexibility and mechanical support in plants?
questions.explanation: Collenchyma tissue provides flexibility and mechanical support, especially in growing parts like young stems and leaves. Sclerenchyma provides rigid support (hard and non-flexible), xylem transports water, and phloem transports food.

questions.question: Who has been appointed as the Chairperson of the National Commission for Protection of Child Rights (NCPCR)?
questions.explanation: Valeti Premchand was appointed as the Chairperson of the National Commission for Protection of Child Rights (NCPCR) in December 2025. Earlier chairpersons include Priyank Kanoongo and Tripti Gurha (2025) before the current appointment. About NCPCR: It is a statutory body established under the Commission for Protection of Child Rights Act, 2005. It works under the Ministry of Women and Child Development. Its main role is to protect, promote, and monitor child rights in India. It ensures proper implementation of laws related to children, such as the Right to Education and protection from abuse.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is true about potential energy?
options: It cannot be converted into kinetic energy
It is always zero at the ground level
It can be negative depending on the reference point.
questions.explanation: Potential energy depends on factors like mass, height, and the reference point, so option A is incorrect. It can be converted into kinetic energy (for example, a falling object), so option B is incorrect Potential energy is not always zero at ground level; the zero level is chosen arbitrarily, so option C is incorrect. Potential energy can be negative depending on the chosen reference level (e.g., gravitational potential energy can be negative below a chosen zero level). Option D is correct.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is Incorrect regarding the Chola dynasty?
options: The Cholas had a powerful navy and engaged in maritime trade
The Brihadeshwara Temple at Thanjavur was built during their rule
Their administration included a well-developed local self-government system
Vanji was the capital city during Chola dynasty
questions.explanation: Vanji was the capital of the Chera dynasty, not the Cholas. Hence, option D is incorrect.

questions.question: Through which method of cell devision are the diploid chromosomes divided into haploid gametes?
questions.explanation: Meiosis is the type of cell division that reduces the chromosome number from diploid (2n) to haploid (n) and produces gametes (sperm and egg cells), so option A is correct. Amitosis is a simple form of cell division without spindle formation and does not produce gametes, so option B is incorrect. Binary fission occurs in prokaryotes like bacteria and is not related to gamete formation, so option C is incorrect. Mitosis produces two identical diploid cells and does not reduce chromosome number, so option D is incorrect.

questions.question: Match the following: Column-I [International Organization] Column-II [Establishment year] A) G-7 1) 1993 B) G-20 2) 1975 C) European Union 3) 1967 D) ASEAN 4) 1999
questions.explanation: G–7 is an informal group of the major developed economies of the world. It was established in 1975. At that time, the world was facing problems such as the oil crisis and economic recession. To deal with these challenges, a meeting of leaders of major developed countries was held at Rambouillet in France. Initially, six countries participated in this meeting. Later, with the inclusion of Canada, the group became a seven-nation grouping and came to be known as the G–7. G–20 is a group of major developed and emerging economies of the world. It was established in 1999 after the Asian financial crisis. The main objective of this forum is to promote global economic stability, strengthen financial cooperation, and ensure coordination in international economic policies. In the beginning, it included the finance ministers and central bank governors of member countries. Later, it was expanded to the level of heads of state and government. European Union is a political and economic union of countries in Europe. It was formally established in 1993 after the implementation of the Maastricht Treaty. Before this, several institutions existed to promote economic cooperation among European countries. These institutions were later integrated into a broader framework to create the European Union. Its main objective is to promote economic integration, political cooperation, and regional stability among member countries. Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) is a regional organization established in 1967 through the Bangkok Declaration. Its founding members were Indonesia, Malaysia, Philippines, Singapore, and Thailand. The primary aim of this organization is to maintain peace in the region, promote economic development, and strengthen cooperation among member countries.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding Poverty Estimation Committees in India: • The Tendulkar Committee removed the calorie-based poverty line and included health and education expenditure. • The Lakdawala Committee recommended uniform poverty line for all states without using price indices. • The Rangarajan Committee suggested a higher poverty line compared to the Tendulkar Committee. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct- The Tendulkar Committee (2009) changed the methodology of poverty estimation. It moved away from the calorie-based approach and adopted a broader consumption expenditure method. This method included essential expenses such as health and education, making the estimation more realistic. Statement 2 is incorrect- The Lakdawala Committee (1993) did not recommend a uniform poverty line for all states. Instead, it suggested state-specific poverty lines because price levels differ across states. It also recommended the use of Consumer Price Index (CPI) to update poverty lines. Therefore, the statement is incorrect. Statement 3 is correct- The Rangarajan Committee (2014) revised the poverty line and set it at a higher level compared to the Tendulkar Committee. This led to a higher estimation of the poor population, as the minimum consumption threshold was increased.

questions.question: Where is the HQ of World Squash Federation?
questions.explanation: The World Squash Federation (WSF), which is the international governing body for squash, has its headquarters in Hastings, United Kingdom. Here is the list of some major sports’ Headquarters. • International Olympic Committee (IOC) HQ: Lausanne, Switzerland • FIFA (Football) HQ: Zurich, Switzerland • International Cricket Council (ICC) HQ: Dubai, UAE • International Hockey Federation (FIH) HQ: Lausanne, Switzerland • Badminton World Federation (BWF) HQ: Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia • International Tennis Federation (ITF) HQ: London, UK • World Athletics HQ: Monaco • International Table Tennis Federation (ITTF) HQ: Lausanne, Switzerland • World Aquatics (formerly FINA) HQ: Lausanne, Switzerland • Union Cycliste Internationale (UCI) HQ: Aigle, Switzerland • International Shooting Sport Federation (ISSF) HQ: Munich, Germany • International Boxing Association (IBA) HQ: Lausanne, Switzerland • International Golf Federation (IGF) HQ: Lausanne, Switzerland • International Basketball Federation (FIBA) HQ: Mies, Switzerland • International Gymnastics Federation (FIG) HQ: Lausanne, Switzerland • International Volleyball Federation (FIVB) HQ: Lausanne, Switzerland • World Rugby HQ: Dublin, Ireland • World Archery Federation HQ: Lausanne, Switzerland • International Judo Federation (IJF) HQ: Budapest, Hungary • World Taekwondo HQ: Seoul, South Korea • International Ski and Snowboard Federation (FIS) HQ: Oberhofen, Switzerland • International Skating Union (ISU) HQ: Lausanne, Switzerland • World Squash Federation HQ: Hastings, United Kingdom • International Handball Federation (IHF) HQ: Basel, Switzerland • World Baseball Softball Confederation (WBSC) HQ: Lausanne, Switzerland • International Canoe Federation (ICF) HQ: Lausanne, Switzerland • International Rowing Federation (FISA) HQ: Lausanne, Switzerland • International Fencing Federation (FIE) HQ: Lausanne, Switzerland • World Karate Federation HQ: Madrid, Spain • International Weightlifting Federation (IWF) HQ: Lausanne, Switzerland

questions.question: Which of the following deficit included only such transactions that affect the current income and expenditure of the government?
questions.explanation: Revenue Deficit refers to the difference between the government’s revenue expenditure and revenue receipts. It includes only those transactions that affect the current income and expenditure of the government, such as salaries, subsidies, interest payments, taxes, etc. Primary Deficit = Fiscal Deficit − Interest Payments Fiscal Deficit includes total expenditure (revenue + capital), so it is broader Trade Deficit relates to imports and exports, not government accounts

questions.question: Khecheopalri Wetland, which was recognized as a Ramsar Site in 2025, is located in which of the following states?
questions.explanation: Khecheopalri Wetland is a sacred lake located in Sikkim. It was designated as a Ramsar Site in 2025 and is known for its ecological as well as cultural significance, especially among Buddhist communities.

questions.question: International Sex worker’s day observed annually in India on which date?
questions.explanation: International Sex Workers’ Day is observed on 2 June every year. It commemorates a protest by sex workers in France in 1975, where they demanded better working conditions and rights. The day is recognized globally, including in India, to highlight the rights, dignity, and safety of sex workers.

questions.question: A and B start running from the same point on a circular track of 1.5 km. They run in the same direction with speeds of 18 km/h and 23 km/h, respectively. How many times will they cross each other if they continue running for 40 minutes?
questions.explanation: Relative speed of A & B= (23 -18) =5km/h B will cross A after every= 1.5/5 h = 0.3 h =18 minutes in 40 min, they will cross each other = 40/18 = 2 times (ignore fraction)

questions.question: If x=√6+2√3 and y=√6-2√3 , find the value of (x+y)/(x-y) .
If a sum of money amounts to ₹6500 at the end of 3 years and to ₹8500 at the end of 7 years at simple interest, find the rate of interest p.a.
The LCM of A and B, if A = 2³ × 3² × 7³ and B = 2² × 3 × 7⁴, is 2³ × 3² × 7⁴.

A trader bought 360 kg of rice; some part at ₹21 per kg and the rest at ₹25 per kg. He sold all part at ₹30 per kg. If the quantity bought at ₹21 per kg was twice that bought at ₹25 per kg, find the profit or loss percentage incurred by the trader.
The radius of a circular metal plate of radius 21 cm increases by 1.3% under scorching sun. What is the approximate percentage increase in its total area?
questions.explanation: Increase in radius = 1.3% So, increase in its area will be equal to successive increase of 1.3% & 1.3% = (2.6 + 0.0169) % = 2.6169% ≈ 2.62%

questions.question: In a circle, there are two parallel chords of lengths 14 cm and 48 cm. The distance between them is 17 cm. Find the diameter of the circle if both cords are situated on the same side of the center of the circle.
questions.explanation: Short trick: Data are set according to triplet - (7, 24, 25) So, r² = 25 cm & diameter 2r = 50cm OR, Let, radius = r cm & distance b/w center of the circle & longer cord is x cm. So, r² = 24² + x² = (17+x)² + 7² or, 24x = 576 - 289 - 49 or, 24x = 238 x = 7 So, r² = 24² + 7² ∴ r = 25 cm diameter 2r = 50cm

questions.question: A square-based tent has a shape of a pyramid. Side of the base is 12 m and slant height of the tent is 8 m. If the base is also covered, what is the total area of canvas used to build the tent?
questions.explanation: Question asked about total surface area of the pyramid-shaped tent. ∴ Total surface area = Lateral surface area + Base area = (½ × Perimeter of base × slant height) + Base area = (½ × 4 × 12 × 8 + 12²) m² = 192 + 144 = 336 m²

questions.question: If the selling price of 20 items is equal to the cost price of 24 items, what is the profit or loss percentage?
A:B=3:4,B:C=5:6 and C:D=9:11. Find A:D.
A beaker contains 225 ml pure acid. How much water must be added to it to obtain a solution of 45% acid?
questions.explanation: Acid : Water 45% : 55% or, 9 : 11 ∴ Quantity of water must be added - (225/9 × 11) ml = 275 ml

questions.question: If secC-tanC=1/(√5), then find secC×tanC.
questions.explanation: Given, sec C - tan C = 1/√5 —— (i) Now, sec² C - tan² C = 1 or, (sec C − tan C) × (sec C + tan C) = 1 Thus, sec C + tan C = √5 —— (ii) By solving (i) & (ii) → sec C = 3/√5 & tan C = 2/√5 Thus, sec C × tan C = (3 × 2)/(√5)² = 6/5

questions.question: A vertical tower of height 45 m casts a 70 m shadow. At the same time a nearby building casts a 49 m shadow. What is the height of the building?
questions.explanation: According to similarity of two triangle - Thing ÷ Its shadow = 45/70 = Building/49 ∴ Height of the Building = 45/70 × 49 = 63/2 = 31.5 m

questions.question: In a triangle PQR, the orthocenter is H and incenter is I. If ∠QIR - ∠QHR = 60 ̊, then what is the value of ∠P?
The distance between the centers of two circles of radii 15 cm and 6 cm is 8 cm. How many common tangents can be drawn to these two circles?
questions.explanation: Radii of two circles, r₁ = 15cm, r₂ = 6 cm and distance b/w their centers, D = 8cm As, D = 8cm So NO COMMON TANGENT can be drawn, i.e., Answer is [0]

questions.question: A train crosses a man standing on a platform in 15 seconds and crosses the platform, which is 180 meters long, in 20 seconds. What is the length of the train?
questions.explanation: Let, assume, length of the train = x meters ∴ Speed of the train = x/15 = ((x+180))/2.0 or, 4x - 3x = 540 or, x = 540m

questions.question: The average of consecutive even natural number from ‘N’ to ‘3N’ is 52. What is the value of ‘2N’?

Pipe A can fill a tank in 24 hours and pipe B can empty the same tank in 20 hours. In how many hours will the tank be completely emptied if the both the pipes are opened simultaneously when 1/5th of the tank is already filled with water?
questions.explanation: Pipe A (inlet) - Time: 24h, Capacity of Tank: 5 unit/h, Efficiency Pipe B (outlet) - Time: 20h, 120-unit, -6 unit/h Now, 1/5 th of the tank capacity = 120 × 1/5= 24 unit is already filled ∴ 24-unit water will be emptied in - 24/(5-6) = [24h]

questions.question: The decimal expansion of √8 is:
questions.explanation: √8 = 2√2 is an irrational number, so decimal expansion of √8 will be non-terminating and non-repeating.

questions.question: If the median of the data set: 4, 5, 8, x+4, 2x+1, 21 is 10, what is the sum of the 4th and the 5th term.
questions.explanation: 4, 5, 8, (x+4), (2x+1), 21 (6 terms) Median will be the average of 3rd & 4th term → (8 + x + 4)/2 = 10 or, x = 8 ∴ Sum of the 4th & the 5th term = (x+4) + (2x+1) = 3x + 5 = 24 + 5 = 29

questions.question: The angle subtended by a cord at the center of a circle is 90 ̊. What is the angle subtended by the same cord at the circumference?
questions.explanation: Angle subtended by the chord at the center = 90° ∴ Angle subtended by it at the circumference = 90°2 = 45°

questions.question: How many 4-digit numbers can be obtained by using digits 0, 2, 3, 5, 6 if repetition of digits is allowed?
questions.explanation: While forming 4-digit numbers by using 0, 2, 3, 5, 6 when repetition is allowed, we cannot use '0' at thousands place. So total number of 4-digit numbers will be - 4 × 5 × 5 × 5 = 500

questions.question: Preeti took ₹20400 as a loan which is to be paid with an interest rate of 12.5% p.a. compounded annually in two equal installments. Find the value of each installment.
questions.explanation: Principle installment 1st Year = (8 9) × 9 [∴ +12.5% = + 1/8 ] 2nd Year = (64 81) Total principle = (8×9 + 64) = 136 unit Value of each installment = (20400×81/136) Rupees = 12150 Rupees

questions.question: For a certain set of data, variance=0.09, mean=36 and median=40. Find the Karl Pearson’s coefficient of skewness.
questions.explanation: Variance = 0.09 ∴ Standard Deviation (SD) = √0.09 = 0.3 ∴ Karl Pearson's coefficient of skewness (Sₖ) = (Mean - Mode)/SD or 3(Mean - Median)/SD ∴ Sₖ = [3(36-40)]/0.3= (-12)/0.3 = -40

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
options: Unless the local authorities intervene, there
is likely to be numerous protests regarding
the new land acquisition policy in the state.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option (B) 👉 Replace "is" with "are" 📌 Error concept: The error involves the inconsistency in Subject-Verb Agreement with introductory subject "There". Grammatical concept: In sentences starting with "There", the word "there" is an introductory subject (dummy subject), and the real subject follows the verb. The verb must agree with that following real subject. In this sentence, the real subject is "numerous protests" (Plural). Therefore, the verb must be plural (are).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Even in the face of extreme adversity, the young athlete managed to __________ her dream of winning an Olympic gold medal.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (4). 📌 Explanation - Hold on to means to keep something that is important to you, or to continue to believe in an idea or a goal despite difficulties. In the given sentence, the athlete refuses to give up her goal of winning a medal despite extreme adversity. • Hold on to: थामे रखना / बनाए रखना - To maintain a grip on something or to keep a belief/hope. Example: You should hold on to your savings in case of an emergency. • Hold out for: बेहतर की प्रतीक्षा करना - To wait for something better or specific; to refuse to accept anything less than what you want. Example: The workers are holding out for a 10% pay rise and refuse to return to work until then. • Hold back from: हिचकना / रोकना - To hesitate or to prevent yourself from doing something. Example: She had to hold back from crying when she heard the news. • Hold up with: This is not a standard idiomatic phrasal verb. • Usually, we use "Hold up" which means to delay or support, or "Keep up with" which means to stay at the same level. Example: Sorry I'm late; I got held up in traffic.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. The startup failed within six months due to a paucity in capital and experienced mentors.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C) paucity of. 📌 Explanation - The noun "paucity" refers to the presence of something in only small or insufficient quantities or amounts. In English grammar, it is a standard rule that "paucity" is followed by the fixed preposition "of." Using "in," "for," or "with" is grammatically incorrect in this context. Paucity - कमी / अल्पता (Scarcity) - The presence of something in only small or insufficient quantities. Example: There is a paucity of evidence to support your claim in court.

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: The committee was comprised of ten members, including three external experts.
The company is indifferent to the feedback provided by its disgruntled employees.
Despite of the heavy rain, the match continued until the final whistle blew.
She has been residing at Mumbai for the last ten years.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 📌 Error Concept - Options A, C, and D contain classic prepositional errors frequently tested in SSC exams. Option B correctly uses the fixed preposition "to" with the adjective "indifferent." Grammatical Concept - Let's break down why the other options are incorrect and how to fix them 1. Option (A): The error of "Comprised of". 👉 Replace: "comprised of" with "composed of" or simply "comprised". Rule: The verb Comprise means "to consist of." It is never followed by "of" in the active voice. However, "Composed" is always followed by "of." 2. Option (B): Correct Usage- Rule: The word Indifferent (meaning: having no particular interest or sympathy; unconcerned) always takes the fixed preposition "to". 3. Option (C): The error of "Despite of" 👉 Replace: "Despite of" with "Despite" or "In spite of". Rule: Despite is a preposition that never takes "of." Only In spite requires "of." 4. Option (D): The error of "At" vs "In" for large cities 👉 Replace: "at Mumbai" with "in Mumbai". Rule: We use "at" for small, specific points or small towns/villages, but "in" is used for large cities, states, and countries.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. The integration of cutting-edge technologies into the healthcare industry have the prospect of revolutionising the way medical professionals provide patient care.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C)- has the prospect 📌 Explanation - The underlined part "have the prospect" is grammatically incorrect because the subject of the sentence is the singular noun "integration." In English grammar, the verb must always agree with the main subject, not the nouns in the prepositional phrases that follow it (like "technologies" or "industry"). Therefore, the plural verb "have" must be replaced by the singular verb "has." Why the other options are wrong - 1) Option (A) have the potential: This option is grammatically incorrect. Although "potential" is a great synonym for "prospect," the verb used here is "have" (plural). Since our subject "integration" is singular, this option fails the basic Subject-Verb Agreement test. 2) Option (B) were the potential: This option is incorrect for two reasons: A.) Tense/Number: "Were" is a plural past tense verb. The sentence describes a current, ongoing fact about technology, so it should be in the Present Tense. Even if it were past tense, for a singular subject, we would use "was." B.) Logic: "Integration was the potential" sounds like the integration was the possibility itself, rather than having a possibility.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. The board of directors decided to ___________ the proposed merger after realizing it would not be profitable in the long run.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D) - Annul 📌 Explanation - The sentence states that the board realized the merger would not be profitable. Therefore, they would naturally want to cancel or declare it invalid. "Annul" is the most appropriate formal and legal term used for cancelling a merger, contract, or marriage. Meanings and Examples of All Options: • Adapt - अनुकूल बनाना / परिस्थिति के अनुसार ढालना - To adjust or modify something to fit a new use or situation. Example: It took the new employees some time to adapt to the company's working culture. • Adopt - अपनाना / गोद लेना / स्वीकार करना - To legally take something as one's own or to formally accept a suggestion or policy. Example: The committee decided to adopt the new safety guidelines immediately. • Abjure - शपथपूर्वक त्यागना / सार्वजनिक रूप से छोड़ना - To solemnly renounce or give up a belief, cause, or claim (usually used for religion or loyalty). Example: The prisoner was forced to abjure his allegiance to the rebel group. • Annul - रद्द करना / अमान्य घोषित करना - To declare officially that something (like a marriage, contract, or law) is invalid and no longer exists. Example: The higher court moved to annul the previous judgment due to lack of evidence.

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
options: The manager speaks to the staff
as if he was the owner of
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option (B) 👉 Replace "was" with "were" 📌 Error concept: This is an error of Subjunctive Mood. In sentences expressing an imaginary, hypothetical, or contrary-to-fact condition (usually starting with if, as if, as though, I wish), we use the plural helping verb "were" regardless of the subject being singular or plural. Grammatical concept: The Subjunctive Mood is used to explore hypothetical situations or to express wishes and demands. When using "as if" or "as though" to describe a situation that is not true (the manager is not actually the owner), the subjunctive form "were" is required. Structure: Subject + Present Indefinite (V1/V5) + as if / as though + Subject + V2 (Subjunctive)

questions.question: Select the sentence containing the homonym of the highlighted word: The suitcase was so compact that it easily fit into the overhead compartment of the airplane
options: The city’s downtown area is very compact, making it easy to explore on foot.
Before the construction began, the workers had to compact the loose gravel.
The two nations decided to enter into a compact to reduce carbon emissions.
She preferred a compact car because it was much easier to park in the city.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C) 📌 Explanation - This is a "Homonym" question. Homonyms are words that have the same spelling and pronunciation but different meanings. In the Question: "Compact" is used as an Adjective which means something that is small and neatly packed together, or a small case containing face powder and a mirror (छोटा और सुगठित / श्रृंगार-दान). In Option C: "Compact" is used as a Noun which means a formal agreement or contract between two or more parties (समझौता / इकरारनामा). This is a different grammatical category and a completely different core meaning, making it the correct homonym choice. WHY OTHER OPTIONS ARE WRONG: All other options (A, B, and D) use COMPACT as an adjective or verb that preserves the same core meaning of "being dense, closely packed, or small" as the highlighted word in the main sentence.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. A. In this style of essay, it is critical to incorporate precise details that allow the reader to experience the emotion. B. When creating a narrative essay, you should have a cast of people, a setting, a compelling storyline and a satisfying ending. C. Not only should they express their emotions or employ their senses, but they should also allow the tale to convey a point. D. A narrative essay tells a tale from a certain point of view or can say a story of the personal experiences one had.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct option is (A) DBAC 📌 Explanation - This sequence follows a logical progression from the definition of the essay to its components, and finally to the finer details of execution. 1.) Opening Sentence (D): This sentence introduces the subject by defining what a "narrative essay" is (telling a tale or personal experience). It sets the stage for the entire paragraph. 2.) Structural Components (B): After defining the essay, sentence B lists the necessary "ingredients" required to build one: a cast, setting, storyline, and ending. It moves from the concept (D) to the physical structure (B). 3.) Qualitative Requirement (A): Once the structure is set, sentence A adds a layer of depth, stating that it is "critical" to include precise details so the reader can feel the emotions. 4.) Final Goal (C): Sentence C concludes the thought by emphasizing that while emotions are important, the ultimate purpose is for the tale to "convey a point." Final Paragraph - A narrative essay tells a tale from a certain point of view or can say a story of the personal experiences one had. When creating a narrative essay, you should have a cast of people, a setting, a compelling storyline and a satisfying ending. In this style of essay, it is critical to incorporate precise details that allow the reader to experience the emotion. Not only should they express their emotions or employ their senses, but they should also allow the tale to convey a point.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. The court let the plaintiff argue the motion without interruption.
options: The motion was argued by the plaintiff without interruption
The plaintiff was let to argue the motion without interruption
The plaintiff was allowed to argue the motion without interruption
The court was letting the plaintiff argue the motion
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C). 📌 Explanation- The sentence "The court let the plaintiff argue..." uses the verb let. While "let" can be used in passive voice (Option B), it is grammatically awkward and rarely used in formal English or competitive exams like SSC CGL. The standard practice is to replace let with allow when converting to the passive voice to maintain better sentence structure. Grammatical Concept: In Active to Passive conversion, the verb let follows special rules: Direct Passive (Rare): Subject + be + let + V1 (without 'to'). Example: He let me go. → I was let go Standard Passive (Preferred): Use the verb allow + to-infinitive. • Structure: Subject + was/were + allowed to + V1. • Example: The teacher let us leave. → We were allowed to leave by the teacher.

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. He mentioned that he had been working on the project all night before the deadline shifted.
options: "I have been working on the project all night before the deadline shifted," he said.
"I was working on the project all night before the deadline shifts," he said.
"I had been working on the project all night before the deadline shifted," he said.
"I am working on the project all night before the deadline shifted," he said.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C). 📌 Explanation - When a sentence in Indirect Speech is in the Past Perfect Continuous tense (had + been + V1+ing), and it refers to a specific completed action in the past (like a deadline shifting), the Direct Speech often retains the same tense if the action was already in the "past of the past" when spoken. Grammatical Concept In Narration, the conversion of tenses follows specific rules. However, there is a special rule for the Past Perfect and Past Perfect Continuous: • Past Continuous (was/were + ing) changes to Past Perfect Continuous (had + been + ing). • Past Perfect Continuous (had + been + ing) remains Unchanged when converting from Direct to Indirect. Since the original sentence uses "had been working," the most accurate direct representation of that specific state—especially when linked to another past action ("shifted")—is the Past Perfect Continuous itself. This indicates the work was already ongoing and completed relative to the deadline change.

questions.question: The "India Employment Report 2024" paints a grim picture of the country’s widely touted "demographic dividend," which appears set to waste away ___(1)___there are immediate and targeted policy interventions. The report highlights that youth account for almost 83% of India's unemployed workforce. Even more distressing is the fact that the proportion of educated young people among the unemployed has almost doubled. These depressing statistics underline an absence of jobs capable of ___(2)___educated youth. Furthermore, wages have either stagnated or witnessed a decline after accounting for inflation. ____(3)___ , the window for India to leverage its sizeable cohort of youth for socio-economic gains is fast closing. While the economy has been growing at a high rate, working conditions ___(4)___ a majority of employed youths remain poor. Policy experts argue that the government must accord primacy to job creation ___(5)___ ensures the quality of education and training for a technologically evolving economy. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B) unless. 💢 Explanation - The sentence presents a negative condition: the demographic dividend will be wasted if not for immediate policy interventions. "Unless" means "if... not" and fits the logical flow perfectly. Why other options are wrong: • since: This implies a reason (because), which would mean the dividend is wasting because of interventions, which is logically backwards. • although: This expresses contrast, but the sentence is meant to show a condition for success or failure. • D) whether: This is used to express a choice between two possibilities (if or not), but here we need a specific condition.

questions.question: The "India Employment Report 2024" paints a grim picture of the country’s widely touted "demographic dividend," which appears set to waste away ___(1)___there are immediate and targeted policy interventions. The report highlights that youth account for almost 83% of India's unemployed workforce. Even more distressing is the fact that the proportion of educated young people among the unemployed has almost doubled. These depressing statistics underline an absence of jobs capable of ___(2)___educated youth. Furthermore, wages have either stagnated or witnessed a decline after accounting for inflation. ____(3)___ , the window for India to leverage its sizeable cohort of youth for socio-economic gains is fast closing. While the economy has been growing at a high rate, working conditions ___(4)___ a majority of employed youths remain poor. Policy experts argue that the government must accord primacy to job creation ___(5)___ ensures the quality of education and training for a technologically evolving economy. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A) absorbing 💢 Explanation - In an economic context, when an industry or market provides jobs for people, it is said to be "absorbing" the workforce. The sentence means there is a lack of jobs capable of hiring or taking in educated youth. Why other options are wrong: • B) discarding: This means throwing away or rejecting; the problem isn't that jobs are rejecting youth, but that the jobs aren't there to take them in. • C) exhausting: This means using up completely or tiring out, which doesn't fit the context of job creation. • D) mitigating: This means to make something less severe (like a problem). You don't "mitigate" youth; you mitigate unemployment.

questions.question: The "India Employment Report 2024" paints a grim picture of the country’s widely touted "demographic dividend," which appears set to waste away ___(1)___there are immediate and targeted policy interventions. The report highlights that youth account for almost 83% of India's unemployed workforce. Even more distressing is the fact that the proportion of educated young people among the unemployed has almost doubled. These depressing statistics underline an absence of jobs capable of ___(2)___educated youth. Furthermore, wages have either stagnated or witnessed a decline after accounting for inflation. ____(3)___ , the window for India to leverage its sizeable cohort of youth for socio-economic gains is fast closing. While the economy has been growing at a high rate, working conditions ___(4)___ a majority of employed youths remain poor. Policy experts argue that the government must accord primacy to job creation ___(5)___ ensures the quality of education and training for a technologically evolving economy. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 3 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B) Consequently 💢 Explanation - This is a result-oriented connector. The previous sentences list several problems (stagnating wages, high unemployment). As a result of these problems, the window of opportunity is closing. Why other options are wrong: • Conversely: This is used to show a complete opposite idea, but the second part of the sentence is a direct result of the first part. • Nevertheless: This means "despite that," used for contrast. The sentence isn't contrasting; it’s concluding. • Incidentally: This is used to add a random or less important point (by the way), whereas the closing window is a major, direct point.

questions.question: The "India Employment Report 2024" paints a grim picture of the country’s widely touted "demographic dividend," which appears set to waste away ___(1)___there are immediate and targeted policy interventions. The report highlights that youth account for almost 83% of India's unemployed workforce. Even more distressing is the fact that the proportion of educated young people among the unemployed has almost doubled. These depressing statistics underline an absence of jobs capable of ___(2)___educated youth. Furthermore, wages have either stagnated or witnessed a decline after accounting for inflation. ____(3)___ , the window for India to leverage its sizeable cohort of youth for socio-economic gains is fast closing. While the economy has been growing at a high rate, working conditions ___(4)___ a majority of employed youths remain poor. Policy experts argue that the government must accord primacy to job creation ___(5)___ ensures the quality of education and training for a technologically evolving economy. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 4 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C) - for 💢 Explanation - This is a standard prepositional usage. We say "working conditions for someone." It indicates the specific group (employed youths) experiencing those conditions. Why other options are wrong: • on: This would suggest conditions are physically resting on the youth. • to: This is usually used for direction or relationship (e.g., "kind to someone"), but not for "working conditions." • at: This is used for specific locations, not for a demographic group.

questions.question: The "India Employment Report 2024" paints a grim picture of the country’s widely touted "demographic dividend," which appears set to waste away ___(1)___there are immediate and targeted policy interventions. The report highlights that youth account for almost 83% of India's unemployed workforce. Even more distressing is the fact that the proportion of educated young people among the unemployed has almost doubled. These depressing statistics underline an absence of jobs capable of ___(2)___educated youth. Furthermore, wages have either stagnated or witnessed a decline after accounting for inflation. ____(3)___ , the window for India to leverage its sizeable cohort of youth for socio-economic gains is fast closing. While the economy has been growing at a high rate, working conditions ___(4)___ a majority of employed youths remain poor. Policy experts argue that the government must accord primacy to job creation ___(5)___ ensures the quality of education and training for a technologically evolving economy. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 5 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D) that 💢 Explanation - In this sentence, we need a Relative Pronoun to connect the noun phrase "job creation" to the verb "ensures." The word "that" acts as the subject for the verb "ensures," implying that the process of job creation itself must be of a kind that guarantees or leads to quality education and training. Why other options are wrong: • who: This is a relative pronoun used only for persons. Since "job creation" is a concept/process, "who" cannot be used. • while: This is a conjunction used to show two simultaneous actions. If we used "while," the verb would typically need to be in the continuous form (while ensuring) or have its own subject (while the government ensures). It cannot directly precede a finite verb like "ensures" in this structure. • but: This is a coordinating conjunction used to show contrast. The sentence is not trying to contrast job creation with education; rather, it is linking them as part of a unified policy goal.

questions.question: Identify the word that is correctly spelt.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Condescending Let’s see the meaning of all words: • Surreptitious - गुप्त - secretive or stealthy Example: He made a surreptitious glance at his phone. • Condescending - अवमानना पूर्ण - showing superiority or patronage Example: Her condescending tone annoyed everyone. • Abhorrent - घृणित - disgusting or detestable Example: The idea of hurting animals is abhorrent to her. • Extol - प्रशंसा करना - to praise highly Example: The critics extol the new restaurant's innovative dishes.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence – The town's drab streets made it hard to find excitement.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – uninspiring. Let’s see the meaning of all words: • Drab - सादा - dull and uninteresting Example: The town's drab streets made it hard to find excitement. • Devine - दिव्य - divine or heavenly Example: The devine aroma of freshly baked cookies filled the air. • Atrocious - भयानक - extremely bad or unpleasant Example: The atrocious weather ruined our vacation plans. • Uninspiring - अनोखा नहीं - lacking excitement or interest Example: The movie's uninspiring plot left us disappointed. • Worn - घिसा हुआ - showing signs of wear or damage Example: The worn shoes needed to be replaced.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Purge
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – clutter Let’s see the meaning of all words: • Purge - शुद्ध करना - to remove impurities or unwanted elements Example: The government decided to purge the system of corrupt officials. • Merry - खुश - joyful or cheerful Example: The party was merry and loud. • clutter - अव्यवस्थित करना - to make something untidy Example: The room was cluttered with unnecessary items. • Divulge - प्रकट करना - to reveal a secret Example: She wouldn't divulge the surprise party plans. • Unveil - प्रकट करना - to reveal or show something Example: The new product was unveiled at the launch even.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. Drew on his fancy
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Used his imagination Let’s see the meaning of idiom: Drew on his fancy - अपनी कल्पना का उपयोग करना - used his creativity or imagination Example: The poet drew on his fancy to write the beautiful verse.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. Mammoth
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – gigantic Let’s see the meaning of all words: • Mammoth - विशाल - extremely large or enormous Example: The mammoth task of cleaning the entire house took days. • gigantic - विशालकाय - extremely big or huge Example: The gigantic elephant roamed the savannah. • Fierce - क्रूर - having a strong and unpleasant quality Example: The fierce lion roared loudly. • Limitless - असीम - without any limits or boundaries Example: The possibilities are limitless. • Phantom - भूत - a ghostly or imaginary figure Example: The phantom of the opera haunted the theater.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- The friends enjoyed some light banter during lunch.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Abuse Let’s see the meaning of all words: • Banter - मजाक - good-natured joking or teasing Example: The friends enjoyed some light banter during lunch. • Wistful - इच्छापूर्ण - having a melancholic longing Example: She felt wistful about her childhood memories. • Latch - ताला लगाना - to fasten or secure Example: Please latch the door before leaving. • Glide - आसानी से चलना - to move smoothly Example: The skater began to glide across the ice. • Abuse - गाली-गलौज - insulting or hurtful language Example: The conversation turned from banter to abuse.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. A person who does or studies something but is not serious about it and does not have much knowledge
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Dilettante Let’s see the meaning of all words: • Arsenal - शस्त्रागार - a collection of weapons or a place where they are stored Example: The team's arsenal included several star players. • Feline - बिल्ली से संबंधित - relating to cats Example: The feline species is known for its agility. • Asylum - आश्रय - a place of safety or refuge Example: The refugee sought asylum in the embassy. • Dilettante - शौकिया - A person who does or studies something but is not serious about it and does not have much knowledge Example: He's a dilettante when it comes to painting.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom. After hours of negotiation, he finally gave in to their demands.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Yield Let’s see the meaning of idiom: Give in - हार मानना - to surrender or yield Example: After hours of negotiation, he finally gave in to their demands.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. Displaying great dignity
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Gallant Let’s see the meaning of all words: • Gallant - वीर - displaying great dignity or nobility Example: The gallant knight saved the day. • Kudos - प्रशंसा - praise or honor Example: Kudos to the team for their excellent performance. • Orator - वक्ता - a person who is skilled at public speaking Example: The orator delivered a powerful speech. • Reef - चट्टान - a ridge of rock or coral near the surface of the sea Example: The ship ran aground on the reef.

In this question given below is a statement followed by two assumptions. You have to assume everything in the statement to be true and on the basis of the information given in the statement, decide which of the following suggested assumptions can be logically deduced from the statement. Statement: Country X will host the Olympics next year. Assumptions: I. Country X has the infrastructure to support the event. II. Country X will participate in all the events of the Olympics.
options: Neither Assumption I nor Assumption II is implicit.
Both Assumption I and Assumption II are implicit.
questions.explanation: Assumption 1 is implicit because when a country is hosting the Olympics it generally will have all the infrastructure and facilities. Assumption 2 is implicit as host country get automatic qualification in all the events of the Olympics.

questions.question: Pique is related to Resentment in the same way as Prolix is related to?
questions.explanation: Resentment is synonym of Pique Similarly, Lengthy is synonym of Prolix.

questions.question: Three statements are given, followed by two conclusions numbered I and II. questions.explanation: [Some-No] Key word Both conclusion are individually false Either 1 or 2 follows

questions.explanation: R is son-in-law of S.

questions.question: Post: Clerk cum typist (Contractual) Eligibility Criteria: • Minimum 18 years old • Passes matriculation (10th) with any subject • Should have typing speed of at least 45 WPM. • Sign a bond period of 24 months Case: Rohan is 21 years old and has passed B.sc in Maths. Hi s typing speed is 40 WPM and he wants to work for only 18 months before joining a P.G program. Question: What decision should be made in Rohan’s case?
options: Refer the case to the HR team
Select him provided he improves his typing speed and commits to the bond period.
questions.explanation: Rohan’s Typing speed is 40 WPM which is less than the required 45 WPM Also, Rohan will work only for 18 months whereas bond period is 24 months. Hence, Rohan’s application will be rejected .

Only three people sit between Q and S when counted from the right of Q. Only three people sit between P and U when counted from the left of P. R sits between P and S. questions.question: What day of the week was 28th Feb 2009?
questions.explanation: Calculate number of odd days Till 2000  0 odd days 2001-2009= 9+2=11 odd days (2 leap year) Feb code- 3({7 3 3 6 1 4 6 2 5 7 3 5} code for each month respectively from January to December for Normal year) 28+3+11= 42 ÷ 7 = 0 odd days 0 is code of Saturday. (1- Sunday, 2- Monday……… 0- Saturday) (According to shortcut) Saturday.
